name: MCP Registry (Official)
what_it_does: The official MCP server registry maintained by Anthropic's Model Context Protocol team — a canonical directory of available MCP servers for discovery and installation by agents and developers.
use_cases: Discovering available MCP servers by category or capability
Looking up MCP server installation instructions and configuration
Finding official and community MCP servers for specific use cases
Integrating MCP server discovery into agent tool selection workflows
Checking metadata about MCP servers (versions, maintainers, categories)
not_for: Installing MCP servers (use npm/pip install for that)
Evaluating MCP server quality (use Assay for that)
Custom or private MCP server hosting
best_when: An agent or developer needs to discover what MCP servers exist for a given use case and get their installation/configuration details.
avoid_when: You need quality ratings or security assessments of MCP servers — the registry lists servers but does not evaluate them.

auth.notes: Public read access to registry data requires no authentication. Submission of new servers may require GitHub authentication.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Completely free — official infrastructure maintained by Anthropic
pricing.notes: Free public infrastructure. No cost to query or list servers.

performance.rate_limits: Not prominently documented; standard API rate limits likely apply

known_agent_gotchas: No MCP server for the registry itself — must use REST API or query GitHub directly
Registry data may lag behind actual MCP server releases — always check official repos for latest versions
Server quality and security are not verified by the registry — listing does not imply quality
Rate limits not prominently documented — add conservative backoff for automated queries
Schema may evolve as MCP protocol matures — check for breaking changes
agent_readiness.security_notes: Public read API requires no auth — safe for agents to query without credentials. HTTPS enforced. Maintained by Anthropic team with high reliability.

security_score_components.security_notes: Public read API with no credentials required — minimal security surface. HTTPS enforced. Anthropic-maintained infrastructure.
